definitions: Student setting of educational goals or subgoals and planning for sequencing, timing, and completing activities related to those goals, e.g., 'First, I start studying two weeks before exams, and I pace myself'

Student-initiated efforts to select or arrange the physical setting to make learning easier, e.g., 'I isolate myself from anything that distracts me.' 'I turned off the radio so I can concentrate on what I am doing.'
Student arrangement or imagination of rewards or punishment for success or failure, e.g., "If I do well on a test, I treat myself to a movie."
Student-initiated evaluations of the quality or progress of their work, e.g., "I check over my work to make sure I did it right."
Student-initiated overt or covert rearrangement of instructional materials to improve learning, e.g., "I make an outline before I write my paper."

Student-initiated efforts to secure further task information from nonsocial sources when undertaking an assignment, e.g., "Before beginning to write the paper, I go to the library to get as much information as possible concerning the topic."
Student-initiated efforts to memorize material by overt or covert practice, e.g., "In preparing for a math test, I keep writing the formula down until I remember it."

categories: seeking social assistance from fellow students
definitions: Student-initiated efforts to solicit help from fellow students, e.g., "If I have problems with math assignments, I ask for help in the class forums."
Student-initiated efforts to solicit help from teachers, e.g., "If I have problems with math assignments, I ask a lecturer for help."

categories: seeking social assistance from others (not fellow students or teachers)
definitions: Student-initiated efforts to solicit help from others who are not teachers or fellow students, e.g., "If I have problems with math assignments, I ask a friend to help."

categories: reviewing previously compiled records, e.g. tests, notes or course material/textbooks
definitions: Student-initiated efforts to reread tests, notes, or textbooks to prepare for class or further testing, e.g., "When preparing for a test, I review my notes."
Student-initiated efforts to record events or results, e.g., "I took notes of the class discussion." "I kept a list of the words I got wrong."
Statements indicating learning behavior that is initiated by other persons such as teachers or parents, and all unclear verbal responses, e.g., "I just do what the teacher says."
